MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Set For Trial On Mar-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SECURITAS SECURITY SERVICES USA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Off calendar. Motion already ruled on. See order filed February 18, 2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Amended MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICHAEL KOPPEL INDIVIDUALLY Amended MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL KOPPEL's Amended MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants Securitas Security Services USA and Michael Koppel's motion to strike hearsay statements from the complaint is denied. Paragraphs 19 and 20, including the hearsay statements quoted therein, of the complaint filed by plaintiffs Ameer Ali and Nicholas Kincaid are arguably relevant to their claims for negligent supervision and training. None of the defendants' reasons for striking those paragraphs are persuasive. Contrary to defendants' belief, complaints are rarely read to juries in civil cases, but even if some portions of the complaint is read to the jury, it is unlikely that paragraphs 19 and 20 would be read to the jury. In all events, defendants' appropriate remedy for their concern is to file a motion in limine seeking an order that those paragraphs not be read to the jury. The fact that plaintiffs did not allege more information or context about what they allege in paragraphs 19 and 20 is not grounds to strike those paragraphs. If defendants seek more information from plaintiffs about those paragraphs, they should propound discovery requesting such information. Nor does the court ascribe ill motives to plaintiffs for including paragraphs 19 and 20 in their complaint. Plaintiffs' complaint alleges that defendants committed an array of misconduct against them. Paragraphs 19 and 20, alleging that others experienced similar mistreatment from plaintiffs, are not so far afield from the legal and factual issues raised by the complaint to warrant the striking of those paragraphs or any portions thereof.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Amended MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
